Iran’s Deputy FM Saeed Khatibzadeh: We were on the verge of responding to a grave violation. Pakistan intervened and conveyed messages that helped prevent further escalation. The United States must choose between war and ceasefire — it cannot have both.

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Bloomberg : U.S. President Trump’s top economic adviser, Kevin Hassett, said the 1977 International Emergency Economic Powers Act can be used to impose 50% tariffs on countries supplying weapons to Iran because the U.S. is “in a state of conflict”. The Supreme Court ruled on 20 February that IEEPA does not authorise the president to impose tariffs, without carving out an exception for wartime. The court was split on whether the president has inherent wartime tariff authority. @wfwitness
German Chancellor Merz: We are now resuming talks with Tehran. We are doing this in coordination with the United States and our European partners.
Afaf al-Saidawi and her son Hassan al-Sabbagh victims from yesterday

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇷🇺 🇨🇳 🇹🇼 WSJ : A global scramble is under way to protect submarine cables from potential sabotage. NATO is using ships and drones in the Baltic Sea after a Russia‑linked vessel cut vital cables in 2024. The UK exposed what it called a secret month‑long operation by Russian submarines targeting underwater infrastructure. Taiwan has increased coast guard patrols and penalties for damaging cables after several incidents involving Chinese vessels. The conflict in Iran has raised new concerns that cable‑laying and repair ships may not be able to transit the Strait of Hormuz, delaying fixes. @wfwitness

Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 🇷🇺 According to The Moscow Times, Russian authorities are considering canceling the May 9 Victory Day parades in Moscow and St. Petersburg due to potential threats from new Ukrainian long-range missiles. 📌 Officials warn that even the announcement of a missile threat during the parade would cause major disruption and damage Russia’s image, even if no strike occurs. 🟥 This could mark the first time in 30 years that the parade is not held, with pro-war bloggers citing Ukrainian missiles capable of reaching Moscow within 10–30 minutes. See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

OSINTdefender RT @sentdefender : Today, UK Defense Secretary John Healey announced that Russia has been conducting covert undersea operations near its critical undersea infrastructure north of the nation in the last few months. According to Secretary Healey, the operation involved one Russian Akula-class attack submarine and two Russian submarines associated with Russia’s Main Directorate of Deep-Sea Research (GUGI). According to Healey, a Royal Navy warship and a Royal Air Force P-8 were deployed to monitor all three submarines, after which the Akula-class sub retreated from the area, but according to the secretary, the two GUGI-associated submarines remain on task in the area and are currently being monitored. tweet
Trump:

Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz: Hezbollah, the terrorist organization, is pleading for a ceasefire, and its Iranian patrons are also pressuring and threatening—out of serious fear that Israel will crush Hezbollah. The IDF is prepared and ready to act with force if Iran fires toward Israel.

↩️ Reply to DD Geopolitics: "🇮🇷 Security official: If attacks against Lebanon do not stop, there will be no negotiations A senior security official told Tehran Times: ➡️Stopping the war against Hezbollah has been, and will remain, an important part of Iran’s plan. ➡️If attacks against…" 🇮🇷 Informed source: Iran considers negotiations meaningless without Lebanon being included in a ceasefire ➡️ An informed source told Fars News Agency, referring to speculation about the resumption of negotiations, that “the issue of Lebanon and a ceasefire in that country is a definite and non-negotiable precondition of the Islamic Republic of Iran for entering any new negotiation process.” ➡️ The source added that “Pakistan’s mediating proposal is also based on this,” and said: “Iran’s 10-point plan, which has been communicated to international intermediaries, includes a clear and explicit clause regarding the necessity of including Lebanon as part of a comprehensive ceasefire. Tehran did not accept the ceasefire until the American side and President Trump had officially acknowledged acceptance of the full 10-point package. Therefore, under conditions of continued attacks on Lebanon, it considers negotiations strategically meaningless and unproductive.” ➡️ The source also said that all senior officials of the Islamic Republic of Iran are in full agreement on this issue. This internal consensus means Tehran’s message is unified and clear: if the issue of Lebanon is not resolved, there will be no negotiations. ➡️ The informed source further referred to countermeasures planned in the scenario of a failure to achieve a ceasefire in Lebanon, saying: “The country’s diplomatic and military apparatus does not rely on a single option. It uses multiple options to ensure the inclusion of Lebanon in the ceasefire is secured.” 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Fars News Agency: An informed source said Iran considers negotiations meaningless without Lebanon’s inclusion in a ceasefire. Tehran’s 10‑point plan, transmitted through international mediators, includes an explicit clause requiring Lebanon to be part of a comprehensive truce. Iran only accepted the ceasefire after the U.S. and U.S. President Trump officially acknowledged the entire 10‑point package. All senior Iranian officials are united on this issue: if Lebanon is not addressed, there will be no negotiations. @wfwitness

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Informed source: Iran considers negotiations meaningless without including Lebanon in the ceasefire — An informed source told Fars, referring to speculations about the resumption of negotiations, that "the issue of Lebanon and the ceasefire in this country is an absolute and unchangeable prerequisite of the Islamic Republic of Iran for entering any new negotiation process." — This informed source stated that "the mediatory proposal from Pakistan is based on this principle," and said: "Iran's 10-point plan, which has been communicated to international intermediaries, includes a clear and explicit clause regarding the necessity of including Lebanon as part of a comprehensive ceasefire. Tehran did not accept the ceasefire until the American side and Trump himself officially acknowledged the entirety of this 10-point package. Therefore, under the conditions of continued attacks on Lebanon, it considers negotiations strategically meaningless and futile." — This source also said: All senior officials of the Islamic Republic of Iran are unanimous on this issue. This internal consensus means that Tehran's message is unified and clear: if the Lebanon issue is not resolved, there will be no negotiations. — The informed source further addressed the countermeasures planned in the scenario of the ceasefire in Lebanon not being realized and said: "The country's diplomatic and military apparatus does not rely on a single option. It uses multiple options to ensure the inclusion of Lebanon in the ceasefire is established."

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇱🇧 Israel Hayom: Israel is considering halting its strikes in Lebanon and returning to the pre‑“Roaring Lion” operational posture to help the U.S. begin negotiations with Iran. The move would allow Israel to continue acting against immediate threats and rocket fire, but with a reduced scope. There is no demand for Israeli withdrawal from territory captured in recent weeks. “Israel has actually carried out most of the targeted eliminations planned for this operation during yesterday’s intense strikes, and can therefore accept the proposed limitation,” political sources told the publication. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 The IDF Arabic spokesman Avichay Adraee claimed that Hezbollah has fired approximately 3,000 mortar shells and rockets from south of the Litani River since the start of the current operation, from villages including Bint Jbeil, Yater, Qalila, Bra’shit and Majdal Zoun. This comes despite the Lebanese army declaring on 8 January 2026 that it had completed the disarmament of Hezbollah south of the Litani. The IDF called that announcement an “absolute lie” and evidence of the complete failure of the disarmament process. The spokesman also noted the Lebanese army’s inability to prevent Hezbollah members from crossing the Litani via main bridges and crossings. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇩🇪 🇺🇸 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 German Chancellor Merz announced that Germany will support U.S. efforts to secure freedom of shipping on the Strait of Hormuz after a phone call with NATO Secretary General Rutte. “I have assured him at the same time that Germany will help to secure free shipping on the Strait of Hormuz after a peace treaty, if there is a mandate and a viable concept for it. This message was also conveyed yesterday by General Secretary Mark Rutte in Washington. We had also agreed on this beforehand and we will be on the phone again this evening.” @wfwitness

🇷🇺 🇮🇷 ☢️ Russia to evacuate more staff from Iran nuclear plant despite ceasefire. The head of Russia’s state nuclear giant Rosatom says the company hasn’t reversed its decision to evacuate its staff from Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant, despite the ceasefire. Of 639 Rosatom staff originally at the plant, 611 have been evacuated via Armenia, with around 50 volunteers remaining on site. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇮🇷 Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said Operation “Eternal Darkness” delivered a very powerful blow to Hezbollah, leaving the group stunned and confused. More than 200 alleged members were eliminated yesterday, bringing the total killed in the current campaign to over 1,400 – more than twice the number killed in the Second Lebanon War. Katz stated that Hezbollah is desperate for a ceasefire and its Iranian patrons are pressing and threatening out of deep concern that Israel will crush the group. The IDF is ready to act with force if Iran fires at Israel. The separation of arenas, led by Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, allows Israel to operate against Hezbollah according to a structured plan based on four lines: The border line (destroying Lebanese border villages), the shield line inside Lebanon (expanded from 5 to 15 points), the anti‑tank line (secured through ground manoeuvre), and the Litani line, where the IDF will control the Litani area to prevent infiltrations and the return of residents. @wfwitness
